title: SpaceRole::all() docstring claims automatic inclusion; literal slice is not compiler-enforced
description: The doc comment on SpaceRole::all() says 'A new variant added here is automatically included in both validation (parse) and any error text that calls this function.' This is false for all(). parse and as_str use exhaustive match arms — the compiler enforces their completeness. But all() is a static literal slice: adding a new SpaceRole variant to the enum compiles fine while all() silently omits the new variant. A developer who adds SpaceRole::Owner, fixes the two compile errors in parse/as_str, reads this docstring, and ships — will produce error messages that omit 'owner' from the list of valid roles. Fix: (1) correct the docstring to state that all() must be updated manually; (2) add a test that asserts all().len() == SpaceRole variant count as a runtime regression guard.
close_reason: Fixed docstring to accurately describe manual update requirement; added space_role_all_is_complete test as regression guard
